_comment: Curated qualitative layer the APIs cannot provide. Every entry is cited. Missing entries render as 'not available' on the dashboard - never fabricated. Expanded each refresh.

delivery_remote.evidence: University of Malta offers online/blended degree programmes via its eLearning provision.

funding_government.evidence: University of Malta is primarily state-funded.

intl_dual_degree.evidence: Georgia adopted international (San Diego State) STEM degree partnerships in Tbilisi.

funding_government.evidence: State universities dominate; medical education is a state-steered sector.

MT.text: International enrolment surged to 37% of tertiary students in 2023-24 (up ~27% in one year) on the back of English-medium teaching, competitive fees and post-study work routes; the national education strategy 2024-2030 and Malta's first Professional Doctorate in Education (2024) deepened the offer.

GE.text: Medical education became a growth engine: international students rose from ~17,500 (2021-22) to ~37,100 (2024-25), most in medicine, contributing ~$450M in 2024; reforms tied universities to clinical networks (5 university clinics, 40+ affiliated facilities). A 2024-25 reform proposing to restrict foreign admissions at state universities is a live risk.

missing_features.text: No globally top-ranked research university (best CWUR 2025 rank ~1,622).
Narrow research base relative to advanced EU neighbours; limited PhD-scale STEM depth.

youth_motivators.text: English-medium degrees inside the EU with comparatively low tuition and post-study work rights.
Small, safe Anglophone Mediterranean campus drawing students from 127 countries.

missing_features.text: Research output and global ranking lag advanced neighbours; strength is concentrated in medical training rather than broad R&D.
Policy uncertainty: proposed limits on foreign students at state universities.

youth_motivators.text: Affordable, English-taught medical degrees with early clinical exposure across a wide hospital network.
US-style STEM programmes via international university partnerships in Tbilisi.

institutions.name: Massachusetts Institute of Technology
institutions.feature: STEM, AI and quantum research leader

MT.tuition_note: EU students largely state-funded; non-EU undergraduate fees broadly EUR 10,000-12,000/yr depending on programme.
